name: DNDMind AI Copilot
description: Full-stack AI Dungeon Master copilot with RAG, memory retrieval, structured outputs, tool calling, and deterministic evaluation workflows.
problem: Tabletop role-playing sessions create a lot of changing context: character state, campaign memory, rules references, prior decisions, and narrative continuity. A simple chatbot can respond creatively, but it does not reliably preserve memory, retrieve relevant context, or keep outputs structured enough for repeatable gameplay support.
solution: Built a full-stack AI Dungeon Master copilot that combines application workflow, backend APIs, retrieval, memory, and structured AI responses. The system uses a Next.js interface, ASP.NET Core services, FastAPI inference support, PostgreSQL with pgvector, Docker Compose, embeddings, memory retrieval, tool/function calling, and deterministic evaluation workflows.
proof: Implements RAG and vector retrieval through PostgreSQL + pgvector instead of relying only on prompt history.
Separates frontend, backend, inference, and database responsibilities across a multi-service Docker Compose setup.
Uses structured AI outputs and tool/function calling patterns to make responses easier to validate and integrate.
result: The project demonstrates production-style AI application architecture beyond a prompt-only demo: persistent memory, retrieval, structured outputs, backend orchestration, and evaluation-ready workflows.

name: Enterprise RAG Knowledge Assistant
description: On-premise LLM assistant redesigned into a retrieval pipeline with document chunking, embeddings, vector retrieval, contextual prompts, and output handling.
problem: Internal operational knowledge was spread across documents, systems, and teams. A generic assistant could answer questions, but without retrieval grounding it risked incomplete context, inconsistent answers, and hallucination in an enterprise environment where reliability matters.
solution: Architected an on-premise LLM-powered internal knowledge assistant and redesigned it into a RAG pipeline. The system uses document chunking, embedding generation, vector retrieval, contextual prompt orchestration, backend inference services, and enterprise UI integration.
proof: Designed the assistant for an on-premise enterprise environment rather than a public-only API workflow.
Moved from simple prompting toward retrieval-grounded answers using chunking, embeddings, vector retrieval, and context assembly.
Added controlled response/output handling to reduce hallucination risk and make answers easier to integrate into internal workflows.
result: Improved the architecture of an internal AI assistant from general chat behavior into a more reliable enterprise knowledge workflow with retrieval grounding and clearer backend integration boundaries.

name: LNG Operations Data Pipelines
description: Industrial data pipelines integrating SCADA and FSRU operational systems, replacing Excel workflows and saving about 80 staff-hours per month.
problem: Gas operations data moved through manual spreadsheet workflows and fragmented operational sources. This slowed reporting, increased dependency on human consolidation, and made executive decision-support datasets harder to govern.
solution: Built industrial data pipelines integrating SCADA and FSRU LNG operational systems. The pipelines move multi-source operational data into curated snapshot tables and reporting datasets for dashboards and operational decision support.
proof: Integrated operational systems rather than only web-application databases.
Used Apache Airflow and SQL Server to create repeatable data movement and curated reporting layers.
Connected the resulting datasets to Power BI/Fabric-style executive reporting workflows.
result: Removed manual Excel consolidation for key operational reporting and saved about 80 staff-hours per month while creating a more governed decision-support layer.

name: Executive Reporting Data Store
description: Central SQL Server reporting layer consolidating 30+ internal application databases for KPI dashboards, reporting automation, and safer analytics access.
problem: Internal KPI and reporting needs depended on many separate application databases. Querying production systems directly created reliability, performance, and governance concerns, while manual reporting made executive dashboards slower to maintain.
solution: Created a centralized SQL Server reporting data store that consolidates data copies from 30+ internal application databases. The store acts as a governed reporting layer for Power BI/Fabric dashboards and reporting automation.
proof: Consolidated 30+ internal application databases into a dedicated reporting layer.
Separated analytical/reporting access from direct production database queries.
Supported KPI dashboards and recurring reporting automation for decision-support workflows.
result: Reduced dependency on direct production queries and gave reporting workflows a cleaner, safer data foundation for executive dashboards.

name: 5TB Telco Data to BigQuery
description: Backend synchronization workflows for national-scale modem telemetry into BigQuery, supporting centralized analytics and near real-time reporting.
problem: National-scale modem telemetry needed to be synchronized into a centralized analytics environment. The data volume was large enough that simple exports and manual reporting patterns were not reliable for recurring operational insight.
solution: Designed backend data synchronization workflows that moved 5TB+ modem data into Google BigQuery. The system supported scheduled delivery, centralized analytics, reporting automation, and near real-time operational visibility.
proof: Handled 5TB+ of modem telemetry data.
Used cloud data infrastructure including AWS services and Google BigQuery.
Built scheduled data workflows with tools such as Apache Airflow and Apache Beam.
result: Enabled centralized analytics and more reliable reporting automation for national-scale telco operational data.

name: Nutricia Professional LMS
description: Doctor-facing learning platform for Danone's medical-professional program, delivered on schedule and scaled to thousands of users.
problem: Danone's medical-professional program needed a learning platform for doctors that could launch on schedule and support a large professional audience.
solution: Led a 3-person squad to deliver the LMS, coordinating backend delivery, deployment needs, and production readiness across the project lifecycle.
proof: Built with Next.js, Lumen, MySQL, AWS EC2, and AWS RDS.
Delivered for a real medical-professional audience rather than an internal-only demo.
Coordinated a small engineering squad through launch.
result: Launched on schedule and scaled to thousands of doctors.

name: Inventory Management System
description: Award-winning inventory platform replacing spreadsheet workflows with operational tracking, analytics, and reporting connectors.
problem: Inventory workflows depended heavily on spreadsheets, which made input slower, reporting harder, and operational tracking more error-prone.
solution: Built a web-based inventory management system with structured input flows, operational tracking, analytics support, and reporting connectors.
proof: Implemented as a Laravel/MySQL application with Blade views.
Designed around replacing spreadsheet-driven operational work.
Recognized through the Pertamina Sub-Holding Gas Continuous Improvement Program.
result: Reduced manual inventory handling and created a more reliable platform for operational tracking and reporting.

name: Policy Document Management System
description: Policy document workflow system for managing issued documents, secure transfer, and production document handling.
problem: Policy document handling needed a more reliable workflow for managing generated documents, delivery status, and transfer to downstream systems.
solution: Built a document management service using Next.js, ASP.NET Core, and SQL Server to support structured policy document handling and operational visibility.
proof: Uses a modern frontend with ASP.NET Core backend services.
Stores operational state in SQL Server.
Connects to real insurance document-delivery workflows.
result: Supported faster and more controlled policy document handling, aligned with the E-PDD workflow that reduced issue-to-print time from 24 hours to under 1 hour.

name: E-Procurement Ecosystem
description: Procurement workflow ecosystem covering vendor, procurement, and approval flows for internal business operations.
problem: Procurement processes often involve fragmented requests, vendor coordination, approvals, and audit needs. Without a system, teams depend on manual tracking and scattered communication.
solution: Built an e-procurement ecosystem to centralize procurement workflows, vendor-related processes, and approval visibility.
proof: Implements a business workflow system with CodeIgniter, MySQL, and Bootstrap.
Designed around procurement process flows rather than a simple CRUD application.
Includes repository-accessible implementation details.
result: Created a structured platform for procurement operations and reduced reliance on disconnected manual tracking.

name: Portal Dashboard
description: Gas operations dashboard for internal operational visibility and backend workflow access.
problem: Gas operations teams needed a more centralized portal for operational data, internal workflows, and dashboard access.
solution: Built a gas operations portal using Blazor, ASP.NET Core, SQL Server, and dashboard-oriented UI patterns.
proof: Uses Microsoft-oriented backend and frontend tooling aligned with the enterprise environment.
Connects operational data and internal workflows through a dedicated portal.
Shares implementation through a public repository.
result: Improved operational visibility by giving gas operations users a centralized dashboard and workflow entry point.

name: Tokopedia Scraper API
description: API interface for scraping product data from Tokopedia using Python, Flask, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, and PostgreSQL.
problem: Product research and market analysis can require repeatable extraction of product information from marketplace pages. Manual checking is slow and hard to reuse.
solution: Built a scraper API using Python, Flask, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, and PostgreSQL to expose product scraping through a backend interface.
proof: Combines browser automation and HTML parsing for marketplace data extraction.
Stores extracted data in PostgreSQL.
Exposes the workflow through an API rather than a one-off script.
result: Turned marketplace product extraction into a reusable backend service.
